title: Prism Proof Packet
description: Provider-neutral, machine-readable record of one Prism verification run. Emitted by /prism-verify (and, in reduced form, by plan/build/implement) to .prism/runs/<id>.json. This is the spine every Prism UI renders and every provider adapter produces. It answers the article's six questions: Verified, Evidence, Tests, Assumptions, Risks, Verdict.

schemaVersion.description: Proof Packet schema version. Bump on breaking changes so renderers can adapt.

id.description: Stable run id, e.g. an ISO timestamp + short slug. Names the file .prism/runs/<id>.json.

createdAt.description: When the run completed. Stamped by the host, not invented by the model.

command.description: Which Prism command produced this packet.

task.description: The original task statement being verified — verbatim, not paraphrased.

ref.description: Concrete ref: branch name, commit range (a..b), or PR number/url.

verdict.description: Q6 — the decision. This is the proof layer's whole point.

decision.description: accept = grounded + tests green + no high risk; human-review = uncertain or medium risk; block = a high-severity risk, a failing test presented as done, or a struck load-bearing claim.

rationale.description: One paragraph: why this decision, tied to the risks/tests/assumptions below.

verified.description: Q1 — scope. What was actually examined vs. left out. Honesty about out-of-scope is mandatory.

inScope.description: Concrete things checked (e.g. 'balance-transfer path in ledger.ts', 'auth on the new /pay route').
